ThingDef.label: Mk.13 Mod 6
ThingDef.description:
Caliber: .300 Winchester Magnum. 5-round detachable mag. Bolt-Action.
The Mk.13 is a series of bolt-action sniper rifles that were used by US Navy SEALs (and later, the Marine Corps) starting in the 1990s. All rifles in the Mk.13 series are built off of a Remington 700 long action and are chambered in .300 Winchester Magnum. There are eight rifles in the series (Mod 0-7) with each variant having modifications to the chassis, action, and barrel.
The Mod 0 rifle uses a McMillan A2 chassis, and the Mod 1 rifle is identical except for its left-handed action. The Mod 2 rifle used an Accuracy International AICS chassis instead. The Mod 0/1/2 were later updated with suppressor-ready barrels, with the updated Mod 0 becoming the Mod 3, the Mod 1 becoming the Mod 4, and the Mod 2 becoming the Mod 5. The Mod 6 and Mod 7 rifles switched to a new Accuracy International AX chassis. There were likely never any left-handed versions of the Mod 2/5/6/7.

ThingDef.label: Mk.13 Mod 6 (SD)
ThingDef.description:
Caliber: .300 Winchester Magnum. 5-round detachable mag. Bolt-Action.
The Mk.13 is a series of bolt-action sniper rifles that were used by US Navy SEALs (and later, the Marine Corps) starting in the 1990s. All rifles in the Mk.13 series are built off of a Remington 700 long action and are chambered in .300 Winchester Magnum. There are eight rifles in the series (Mod 0-7) with each variant having modifications to the chassis, action, and barrel.
The Mod 0 rifle uses a McMillan A2 chassis, and the Mod 1 rifle is identical except for its left-handed action. The Mod 2 rifle used an Accuracy International AICS chassis instead. The Mod 0/1/2 were later updated with suppressor-ready barrels, with the updated Mod 0 becoming the Mod 3, the Mod 1 becoming the Mod 4, and the Mod 2 becoming the Mod 5. The Mod 6 and Mod 7 rifles switched to a new Accuracy International AX chassis. There were likely never any left-handed versions of the Mod 2/5/6/7.
